title: July Study Session - Techniques Part 2
overview: Are you interested in learning more about the techniques that are important for all business analysts to know? Please join the IIBA East Tennessee chapter as we continue our study series on the 50 techniques documented in IIBA's Business Analysis Body of Knowledge® (BABOK® Guide).

title: July Study Session - Techniques Part 2
overview: This is a repeat of Part 2 of our 3 part study series reviewing all of the 50 business analysis techniques covered in the Business Analysis Body of Knowledge (BABOK® Guide).

title: August Study Session - Techniques Part 3
overview: This is part 3 of our 3 part study series reviewing all of the 50 business analysis techniques covered in the Business Analysis Body of Knowledge (BABOK® Guide).

description_bullets: Essential skills for business, technical, and security-focused BA roles

overview: Essential skills for business, technical, and security-focused BA roles

title: Business Analysis Certification Study Tips & Strategies
overview: Our East Tennessee IIBA Study Session for October is "BA Certification Study Tips & Strategies" so please make plans to join us. This is a great opportunity to review the steps for preparing for IIBA Business Analyst certification examines, specifically ECBA, CCBA, and CBAP.If you are thinking about getting certified in Business Analysis, or are already planning to take an exam, please join us for this informative session.The session is via Zoom (information below) on Thursday, October 31st from Noon to 1PM Eastern time.

title: Agile Extension to the BABOK - Overview
overview: As part of our continuing monthly study session series, IIBA East Tennessee is pleased to be presenting and overview of IIBA'sAgile Extension to the BABOK Guide. This session will review the guide, which describes the techniques and practices you can use to leverage business analysis skills and an agile mindset to deliver business value.Led byDavid Thomson,this session is scheduled forThursday, November 14th from Noon to 1PM Eastern time.

title: BABOK Study Session: BACCM and BAPM
description_bullets: Business Analysis Core Concept Model (BACCM)
Business Analysis Planning and Monitoring (BAPM)

title: Mastering the Data Dance: A Framework of Data Governance for BAs with Dr. Joe Perez
host: Hosted by the IIBA Bluegrass Chapter
description_bullets: Embracing Integrity
Pacing Infrastructure
Chasing Insight
Facing Impediments
Racing Implementation
Identify key lessons from the Meiji Transformation to inform an adaptable, accountable data governance framework
Develop strategies to foster a culture of continuous improvement and insight-driven decision-making
Formulate a roadmap for implementation and refinement of governance practices that overcome barriers and deliver results
overview: In today’s data-driven world, strong governance is more than an operational necessity—it’s a strategic imperative. In this engaging session, Dr. Joe Perez offers a fresh perspective, drawing surprising and powerful parallels between modern data management and Japan’s Meiji Transformation. Attendees will explore a practical five-part framework built on the following principles: Embracing Integrity Pacing Infrastructure Chasing Insight Facing Impediments Racing Implementation Through real-world stories, strategic insights, and a call to action for data leadership, this session will empower business analysts to shape data governance that delivers lasting impact.
learning_objectives: Identify key lessons from the Meiji Transformation to inform an adaptable, accountable data governance framework Develop strategies to foster a culture of continuous improvement and insight-driven decision-making Formulate a roadmap for implementation and refinement of governance practices that overcome barriers and deliver results
pdu_credits: IIBA® CDU Credit: Earn 1.5 Continuing Development Units (CDUs) for attending this live event. These credits apply toward maintaining your CBAP®, CCBA®, or CBDA® certification. Category: Professional Development | Type: Chapter Event PMI® PDU Credit: Earn 1.5 Professional Development Units (PDUs) for attending this live session. Visit PMI's PDU information page for details.
about_presenter: Dr. Joe Perez is a dynamic thought leader whose career bridges education, IT, and human services. With a doctorate in education and a dual minor in computers and theology, Dr. Perez has held leadership roles at NC State University and the North Carolina Department of Health & Human Services. He is widely recognized for his contributions to the field of data and analytics, earning accolades such as: • Top 10 2024 Thought Leader in IT Strategy & Big Data (Thinkers360) • LinkedIn Top Voice in Data Analytics • IOT Industry Insights 2021 Thought Leader of the Year • Gartner Peer Community Ambassador of the Year A sought-after keynote speaker and author, Dr. Perez brings clarity, creativity, and actionable frameworks to help organizations harness the full potential of their data.

Ideal for: Aspiring or practicing business analysts, early-career professionals, and those seeking a structured path to professional development.

title: Annual General Meeting 2025
description_bullets: 2025 highlights and impact
Financial review and transparency
Board elections
Vision for 2026
Professionals who practice business analysis in any capacity or industry
Individuals interested in the chapter’s progress, governance, and future direction
Those exploring career growth, certification, and mentorship opportunities
Anyone interested in volunteering or future leadership roles
overview: The 2025 Annual General Meeting is your chance to celebrate our chapter’s accomplishments and help shape the future. We’ll review key successes from the past year, present the financial report, and elect the new board. This session will also unveil our strategic vision for 2026. Come ready to engage, share feedback, and connect with peers, sponsors, and chapter leaders as we set the course for an exciting year ahead.
